t: The length of a Valian Year
r: Annals of Aman: 1 V.Y. = 9.582 sun-years, with Tolkien's own arithmetic; NoME's late time-schemes: 1 V.Y. = 144 sun-years.
a: Never reconciled — both are the Professor's own, decades apart. The archive computes with 9.582 and flags the alternative on every pre-Sun date.
w: HoME X (AAm) vs NoME Pt I · shown in the timeline's epoch note

r: Earliest note (WoJ): a son of Finrod's house; published Silmarillion: son of Fingon (an editorial construction CT later regretted); latest manuscript (Shibboleth): son of Orodreth, himself moved to son of Angrod.
a: The latest manuscript layer is Orodreth; the published text is acknowledged editorial. The trees draw Orodreth dashed-[M] and display all three.
w: WoJ; Silm; HoME XII · genealogy node gilgalad

t: Orodreth's own place in the House of Finarfin
r: Published Silm: son of Finarfin, brother of Finrod; Shibboleth: son of Angrod, Finrod's nephew.
a: Late manuscript (nephew) drawn, published variant noted.
w: Silm table I vs HoME XII · genealogy

r: Published Silm: survives to the Third Kinslaying; Shibboleth: burned with the ships at Losgar.
a: Both displayed on his card; no resolution offered.
w: Silm 9/24 vs HoME XII · genealogy node amrod

r: UT main scheme: Sindar of Doriath, grandson of Elmo; a late note: a Teleri of Aman (Galadriel's kinsman there).
a: UT's Sindarin descent drawn [M]; the Aman variant noted. CT himself calls the whole Galadriel-and-Celeborn history 'severely inconsistent'.
w: UT, Hist. of Galadriel & Celeborn · genealogy

t: The history of Galadriel and Celeborn entire
r: UT presents several incompatible schemes of their Second-Age movements (Eregion, Lórinand, the sea-longing), explicitly declining to harmonize.
a: The archive uses only movements attested per-scheme with the scheme named; no composite invented.

t: The date of the Third Kinslaying
r: The Tale of Years of Beleriand revises 532 → 534 → 538 in successive layers.
a: Final layer (538) plotted; the revision chain cited — a correction found by this archive's own audit.
w: WoJ, Tale of Years · timeline y=538

r: Son of Amdír (UT's adopted line); a variant making him son of Galadriel and Celeborn is reported and rejected by CT.
UT prints two traditions side by side: Enerdhil of Gondolin, or Celebrimbor in Eregion (perhaps two stones).
a: Both shown as a fork on the artifact card; no verdict.

r: UT gives three fates side by side — they remained in the East, or perished, or were ensnared by Sauron and became his servants. PoME gives a fourth and opposite account, naming them Morinehtar and Rómestámo, 'Darkness-slayer and East-helper', arriving in the SECOND Age and having 'very great influence… in weakening and disarraying the forces of the East'.
a: Both accounts stated wherever the Istari appear; the later note is later. Tolkien also wrote in 1956 that their names did not exist; a NoME scheme adds Palacendo and Haimenar as Guardians of Cuiviénen. Four accounts, not two.
w: The PoME account is the latest and the most favourable; UT offers its three as alternatives, not as ignorance.

r: The Etymologies' Beleriandic branch is 'Noldorin' (the Exiles' own tongue); the post-LotR re-conception makes Beleriand's tongue Sindarin, adopted by the Noldor.
a: The languages hall files Etymologies forms under Sindarin per the later conception, with the re-basing documented.
w: HoME V vs post-LotR texts · LANGUAGES.md §4

t: The descent of all Elvish speech
r: Lhammas: all tongues descend from Valarin; Quendi & Eldar: Elvish is an independent invention of the Elves.
a: Q&E (later) structures the tree; Lhammas' scheme documented.
w: HoME V vs HoME XI · languages tree

t: Tar-Ardamin in the line of kings
r: UT's Line of Elros includes Tar-Ardamin (Ar-Abattârik); App A's list omits him, making Ar-Adûnakhôr son of Tar-Calmacil — CT judges App A's statement 'probably an error'.
a: UT's fuller line drawn; App A's omission flagged on the node.
w: UT vs LotR App A · genealogy

r: Corrupted Elves (published Silm's frame); corrupted Men, beast-forms, and Maiar-led automata are all weighed in Myths Transformed — Tolkien never settled it.
a: The peoples taxonomy draws the descent-link dashed with the divergence stated.
w: Silm 3 vs HoME X, Myths Transformed · peoples taxonomy

t: Map against text: Angband to Menegroth
r: The Annals say one hundred and fifty leagues; the published map scales to roughly half that.
a: The archive keeps map-consistent geometry and flags the textual figure — a map-text tension, not resolved.
w: CoH intro vs Silm map · RECONSTRUCTION.md

t: Myths Transformed: the Sun and Moon
r: The published cosmology (fruit and flower of the Trees) against the late astronomical re-conception where Sun and Moon precede the Trees' end.
a: The archive's chronology uses the published frame (the corpus's own narrative spine) and records the late alternative here.
w: Silm 11 vs HoME X · timeline epoch note

r: UT's n.36 defines the éored at not less than 120 and the Full Muster at a hundred éoreds — while Théoden's actual musters run smaller; the texts themselves show the ideal against the war-worn real.
a: The armies model uses both: the definition as ceiling, the narrative musters as actuals.
w: UT n.36 vs LotR III · ARMIES.md
